SEARCH
Module storm.core
Package st.orm.core.template.impl

Class SegmentedLruCache<K,V>

java.lang.Object
st.orm.core.template.impl.SegmentedLruCache<K,V>
Type Parameters:
K - key type.
V - value type.
public final class SegmentedLruCache<K,V> extends Object
A sharded LRU cache optimized for concurrent, read-dominated workloads.

This cache partitions entries across multiple independent LRU segments. Each segment is implemented as an access-order LinkedHashMap protected by a segment-level monitor.

Eviction is strict LRU within each segment, and approximate LRU globally (because segments are independent). For many high-hit-rate caches, this yields much higher throughput with minimal complexity and no external dependencies.

Since:
1.8

    Description

    Creates a cache with an automatically chosen segment count.

  • Constructor Details

    • SegmentedLruCache

      public SegmentedLruCache(int maxSize)
      Creates a cache with an automatically chosen segment count.

      The segment count is derived from maxSize using a simple heuristic that targets roughly 128 entries per segment. The resulting segment count is clamped to 4..32 and rounded up to a power of two. This keeps per-segment contention low while maintaining reasonably accurate eviction behavior.

      Parameters:
      maxSize - the maximum number of entries held by the cache across all segments; must be > 0.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if maxSize <= 0.

    • SegmentedLruCache

      public SegmentedLruCache(int maxSize, int segmentCount)
      Creates a cache with a given segment count.

      The supplied segmentCount is rounded up to a power of two. Using a power-of-two segment count makes segment selection fast (bitmask) and keeps distribution stable.

      The total maxSize is split evenly across segments using ceil(maxSize / segmentCount), ensuring the overall cache size bound is respected.

      Parameters:
      maxSize - the maximum number of entries held by the cache across all segments; must be > 0.
      segmentCount - the desired number of segments; must be > 0 (rounded up to a power of two).
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if maxSize <= 0 or segmentCount <= 0.

  • Method Details

    • get

      public V get(@Nonnull K key)
      Returns the cached value for key, or null if no entry exists.

      On a hit, this also updates the entry's recency within its segment, affecting per-segment LRU eviction.

      Parameters:
      key - the key to look up; must not be null.
      Returns:
      the cached value, or null if absent.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if key is null.

    • put

      public void put(@Nonnull K key, @Nonnull V value)
      Stores value under key, overwriting any existing mapping.

      After insertion, the owning segment evicts least-recently-used entries until its segment capacity is respected. Eviction is performed under the segment lock.

      Parameters:
      key - the key; must not be null.
      value - the value; must not be null.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if key or value is null.

    • putIfAbsent

      public V putIfAbsent(@Nonnull K key, @Nonnull V value)
      Stores value under key only if key is absent.

      This method is intended for the "compute outside lock, publish quickly" pattern. It does not call any mapping function and does not perform expensive work while holding the segment lock.

      If the key is present, this method returns the existing value and updates recency (because an internal get is used to check presence).

      Parameters:
      key - the key; must not be null
      value - the value to publish if absent; must not be null
      Returns:
      the existing value if present, otherwise null (and value is stored)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if key or value is null

    • getOrCompute

      public V getOrCompute(@Nonnull K key, @Nonnull Supplier<? extends V> supplier)
      Returns the cached value for key, computing and publishing it if absent.

      This implements a fast read path and a slow compute path:

      1. Attempt get(Object).
      2. If absent, compute the value outside the segment lock using supplier.
      3. Publish using putIfAbsent(Object, Object) to avoid duplicate publication.

      If multiple callers compute concurrently, exactly one computed value is stored. Losers of the race receive the stored (winner) value. This avoids holding locks during computation at the expense of possible duplicate computation under contention.

      Parameters:
      key - the key; must not be null.
      supplier - computes a value when absent; must not be null.
      Returns:
      the cached or computed value, or null if supplier returns null.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if key or supplier is null.

    • segmentCount

      public int segmentCount()
      Returns the number of segments in this cache.

      This is always a power of two and may be larger than the requested segment count passed to the constructor (because it is rounded up).

      Returns:
      the number of cache segments.

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Removes all entries from all segments.

      This method acquires each segment lock in sequence. It is intended for tests and administrative tasks, not for hot paths.

    • remove

      public V remove(@Nonnull K key)
      Removes the mapping for key if present.

      This removal does not affect other keys' recency ordering except for the underlying map mechanics in the owning segment.

      Parameters:
      key - the key to remove; must not be null.
      Returns:
      the removed value, or null if no mapping existed.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if key is null.
